{{Quote|Many Jedi come here to train under Master Zhar.|[[Belaya]]|<ref name="kotor">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
'''Zhar Lestin''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Lethan/Legends|Lethan]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served as member of the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] and the [[Jedi Civil War]]. He taught many students, most notably [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak]], and was among the selected Masters to attend the [[Conclave at Katarr]], when the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was rendered lifeless by [[Darth Nihilus]].

==Biography==
===Mandalorian Wars===
[[File:Revan_Jedi_Council.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Revan defies the council.]]
Before he took the seat on the Council in charge of the Jedi training facility on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], it is known that Zhar spent some time on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where he was often sought out by Revan and Malak for additional training. To his account, Revan was his most promising pupil, one he felt sure would one day be a champion of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. When Revan became ruler of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] instead, he realized he should have recognized as a warning sign his constant lust for knowledge, which he thought was simply eagerness, a common trait for a learner. On Dantooine, Master Zhar went on to teach many students, among them the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Belaya]].<ref name="kotor" />

[[File:CommencementPart6DantooineJediCouncil.jpg|thumb|left|Zhar Lestin sitting on the Jedi Council of Dantooine.]]

When [[Zayne Carrick]] contacted the Enclave from [[The Last Resort (starship)|''The Last Resort'']], seeking help from [[Vandar Tokare]] in the wake of the [[Padawan Massacre]], Zhar was asked by his fellow Council member about any [[Jedi Consular]]s being stationed on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] that might take Zayne under their care and probe his mind to shed some light on the murders he was being accused of. Zhar informed Master Vandar that except [[Lucien Draay]], the Jedi stationed in the [[Jedi Tower]] on Taris were all Consulars, which was the first hint of exposure of the [[Jedi Covenant]].<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 4">[[Knights of the Old Republic 4|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 4]]</ref> Jedi Masters [[Vrook Lamar]] and [[Bala Nisi]], together with Zhar and Vandar, watched him on a news [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] on Dantooine as the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] turned himself in and was brought in chains to his murderous Master by a common [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] in front of angry Tarisians.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 6">[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]]</ref>

===Jedi Civil War===
Years later, after the [[capture of Darth Revan]], the combined action of the Jedi Council erased the mind of the Dark Lord and created new memories for him through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], those of a [[Republic soldier]] under the command of [[Bastila Shan]]. They also destroyed his robe, which was made into a focusing tool of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] energies by alien technology and was viewed as an abomination. Bastila had led the strike team that was sent on [[Darth Revan's flagship|his flagship]], and she was able to convince the Council she could exploit the [[Force-bond/Legends|Force bond]] between her and Revan to draw upon his subconscious memories and find the [[Star Forge]], the means by which he had amassed the forces arrayed against the Jedi.<ref name="kotor" />

Zhar had the chance to teach his former student once again when she returned to the Enclave with Revan after the [[destruction of Taris]]. Except for Master Vrook, he found little objection in the Council when he stated that Revan's [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]], which had already begun to manifest itself through his actions, could not be safely ignored. Revan had little choice in the matter as well, since the Sith would not ignore it either, and [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]] was the only way he could save himself. In a few weeks time under Zhar's tutelage, Revan climbed up the title of Padawan, learning techniques of both [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] and Jedi meditation such as the [[Floating Meditation]] technique. The Twi'lek Jedi Master was amazed at the astonishing rate at which he progressed and would speak of it with folks around the Jedi Enclave, including the Jedi [[Belaya]] and [[Deesra Luur Jada]], and [[Aratech Repulsor Company/Legends|Aratech]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] [[Crattis Yurkal]] and [[Karal Kaar]].<ref name="kotor" />

He then guided Revan through the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]] of apprenticeship, having him display a true understanding of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]], of the construction of a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], and of the lure of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. His final test was the cleansing of an ancient grove on Dantooine from the taint of a fallen Jedi Padawan by the name of [[Juhani]], who was unknowingly undergoing her own trial of the spirit as her Master [[Quatra]] saw best fit for her, and when Revan defeated the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] in battle and showed her the path back to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]], he was made by Master Zhar into a full-fledged Jedi and was presented with the customary [[Jedi apparel/Legends|robes]] of the Order. After that, the Council decreed he and Bastila entered the [[Rakatan ruins (Dantooine)|Rakatan Ruins]] to the east of the Enclave to find the first [[Star Map]] of the [[Star Forge]] [[Star system/Legends|system]], which even Jedi Masters such as [[Nemo]] had been sent to recover without success, leading the Council to think the mission was tied to the destiny of Revan and Bastila, the real threat of dark side corruption notwithstanding.<ref name="kotor" />

Zhar survived the [[Attack on Dantooine (Jedi Civil War)|attack on Dantooine]] by the forces of Darth Malak,<ref name="Vrook">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'': Conversations with the Jedi Masters, particularly [[Vrook Lamar]].</ref> and saw the triumph of his Padawan over the Sith and over the dark side, which he rejected for good even after facing the truth that the Jedi had been using him.

===Death on Katarr===
After the end of the [[Jedi Civil War]], however, Jedi Master [[Atris]] called a [[Conclave at Katarr|council]] on [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]] at which the most powerful members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] were to attend, including Zhar, Vandar and [[Dorak]].<ref name="Vrook" /> They gathered on the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] colony to attempt to find a hidden Sith threat that had been stalking the remnants of the Order in the [[First Jedi Purge]], already thinned dramatically after the war with Revan, Malak, and [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] before them. They were all killed by the very threat that they had gathered to find, along with the Miraluka and all other life on the planet, for [[Darth Nihilus]] could not resist the opportunity to feed on them. Atris had sensed what could happen on Katarr but still didn't warn the Jedi, as she wanted to draw out the Sith; as such she was responsible for their deaths.<ref name="Atris">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'': Conversations with the Jedi Masters, particularly [[Atris]].</ref>
